name: YouTube Publisher
description: Upload and publish videos to YouTube via the YouTube Data API v3

required_variables.description: Google OAuth2 client ID (from Google Cloud Console)
YouTube OAuth2 refresh token (obtained via initial consent flow)

skill.name: youtube_publish
skill.description: Upload and publish videos to YouTube
skill.system_prompt:
You have access to YouTube publishing tools.

Use refresh_youtube_token to get a fresh access token before uploading.
Use upload_to_youtube to upload a video file and set its metadata.

IMPORTANT: For video_url, if the video is a local file (e.g., /tmp/at-compose-XXXXXX/short.mp4), pass the full file path directly — the tool handles local files and URLs.

For YouTube Shorts:
- Videos must be vertical (9:16) and 60 seconds or less
- Include #Shorts in the description for discoverability
- Use category 22 (People & Blogs) for general content
- Keep titles under 100 characters and attention-grabbing
- Add 5-10 relevant tags

tools.name: refresh_youtube_token
tools.description: Refresh the YouTube OAuth2 access token using the stored refresh token

tools.name: upload_to_youtube
tools.description: Upload a video to YouTube with metadata. Call refresh_youtube_token first to get an access_token. Supports both local file paths and URLs for video_url.

video_url.description: Path to the video file (local path like /tmp/at-compose-XXXXXX/short.mp4) or URL to download

title.description: Video title (max 100 chars recommended for Shorts)

visibility.description: Privacy status: public, unlisted, or private. Default: private

tools.handler:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

ACCESS_TOKEN="$ARG_ACCESS_TOKEN"
VIDEO_URL="$ARG_VIDEO_URL"
VISIBILITY="${ARG_VISIBILITY:-private}"
CATEGORY_ID="${ARG_CATEGORY_ID:-22}"

if [ -z "$ACCESS_TOKEN" ] || [ -z "$VIDEO_URL" ] || [ -z "$ARG_TITLE" ]; then
  echo '{"error": "Missing required parameters: access_token, video_url, and title are required"}'
  exit 0
fi

# Create temp directory
WORKDIR="${AT_WORK_DIR:-/tmp}/at-youtube-$(python3 -c 'import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4().hex[:10])')"
mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"
VIDEO_FILE="$WORKDIR/video.mp4"

# Get video file — handle local paths and URLs
if [ -f "$VIDEO_URL" ]; then
  cp "$VIDEO_URL" "$VIDEO_FILE"
elif echo "$VIDEO_URL" | grep -q '^file://'; then
  LOCAL_PATH=$(echo "$VIDEO_URL" | sed 's|^file://||')
  cp "$LOCAL_PATH" "$VIDEO_FILE"
elif echo "$VIDEO_URL" | grep -q '^/'; then
  cp "$VIDEO_URL" "$VIDEO_FILE"
else
  curl -sL -o "$VIDEO_FILE" "$VIDEO_URL"
fi

if [ ! -f "$VIDEO_FILE" ] || [ ! -s "$VIDEO_FILE" ]; then
  echo '{"error": "Failed to get video file"}'
  rm -rf "$WORKDIR"
  exit 0
fi

# Build metadata JSON safely using python3 (avoids shell escaping issues)
METADATA=$(python3 << 'PYEOF' - "$ARG_TITLE" "$ARG_DESCRIPTION" "${ARG_TAGS:-[]}" "$VISIBILITY" "$CATEGORY_ID"
import sys, json

title = sys.argv[1]
description = sys.argv[2]
tags_raw = sys.argv[3]
visibility = sys.argv[4]
category_id = sys.argv[5]

# Parse tags
try:
    tags = json.loads(tags_raw)
    if not isinstance(tags, list):
        tags = []
except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError):
    tags = []

# Ensure #Shorts is in description
if "#Shorts" not in description:
    description += "\n\n#Shorts"

# Strip any system-reminder tags that may have leaked in
import re
title = re.sub(r'<system-reminder>.*?</system-reminder>', '', title, flags=re.DOTALL).strip()
description = re.sub(r'<system-reminder>.*?</system-reminder>', '', description, flags=re.DOTALL).strip()

metadata = {
    "snippet": {
        "title": title[:100],
        "description": description,
        "tags": [str(t) for t in tags][:30],
        "categoryId": category_id
    },
    "status": {
        "privacyStatus": visibility,
        "selfDeclaredMadeForKids": False
    }
}

print(json.dumps(metadata))
PYEOF
)

if [ -z "$METADATA" ]; then
  echo '{"error": "Failed to build video metadata"}'
  rm -rf "$WORKDIR"
  exit 0
fi

# Step 1: Initiate resumable upload
UPLOAD_URL=$(curl -s -D - -o "$WORKDIR/init_body.txt" -X POST \
  "https://www.googleapis.com/upload/youtube/v3/videos?uploadType=resumable&part=snippet,status" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ACCESS_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "X-Upload-Content-Type: video/mp4" \
  -d "$METADATA" | grep -i 'location:' | sed 's/[Ll]ocation: //;s/\r//')

if [ -z "$UPLOAD_URL" ]; then
  INIT_BODY=$(cat "$WORKDIR/init_body.txt" 2>/dev/null || echo 'unknown')
  echo "{\"error\": \"Failed to initiate upload\", \"details\": $(python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.dumps(sys.stdin.read()))" <<< "$INIT_BODY")}"
  rm -rf "$WORKDIR"
  exit 0
fi

# Step 2: Upload the video
UPLOAD_RESP=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -X PUT "$UPLOAD_URL" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ACCESS_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: video/mp4" \
  --data-binary @"$VIDEO_FILE")

HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$UPLOAD_RESP" | tail -1)
RESP_BODY=$(echo "$UPLOAD_RESP" | sed '$d')

rm -rf "$WORKDIR"

if [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "200" ] || [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "201" ]; then
  VIDEO_ID=$(echo "$RESP_BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
  echo "{\"video_id\": \"$VIDEO_ID\", \"status\": \"uploaded\", \"visibility\": \"$VISIBILITY\", \"url\": \"https://youtube.com/shorts/$VIDEO_ID\"}"
else
  echo "{\"error\": \"Upload failed (HTTP $HTTP_CODE)\", \"details\": $(python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.dumps(sys.stdin.read()))" <<< "$RESP_BODY")}"
fi
